zh.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll於大約1220年出生在英國藍卡爾可利黑洛。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1253年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll于大约1220年出生在英国藍卡爾可利黑洛。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1253年之后。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Knoll
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: topographic name for someone living near a hilltop, from Middle English knolle ‘hilltop, hillock’ (Old English cnoll), Middle High German knol ‘peak’. In some cases the English name is habitational, from one of the many places called with this word, for example Knole in Kent or Knowle in Dorset, West Midlands, and elsewhere.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a peasant or a crude, clumsy person, from Middle High German knolle ‘lump, clod’, German Knolle.

variants.name: Knowler
meanings: English (Kent and Sussex): topographic name from Middle English knowle ‘knoll, hilltop’ + the locative suffix -er. See also Knoll .

variants.name: Kniola
meanings: Polish (Knioła): adaptation of German Knoll , Knolle .

variants.name: Knolle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Ernst.
German: variant of   Knoll   1.

variants.name: Knoth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Frieda, Georg.
German: nickname for a gnarled or crude person, from Middle High German knode, knote ‘knot’. Compare Knoll , Knorr .

variants.name: Knull
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhard, Kurt, Manfred.
German: variant of   Knoll   1.

variants.name: Knollman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter.
Americanized form of German Knollmann: topographic name for someone living near the summit of a hill or mountain (see Knoll 1).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll was born about 1220, in Clitheroe, Lancashire, England. He had at least 1 son. He died after 1253.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll est né environ 1220, à Clitheroe, Lancashire, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé après 1253.

de.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll wurde um 1220 in Clitheroe, Lancashire, England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb nach 1253.

it.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll è nato il circa 1220 nel Clitheroe, Lancashire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì dopo il 1253.

ja.Thornley with Wheatley, Clitheroe, Lanca: イギリスランカシュアイアクリスエロー
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Knollは約1220年にイギリスランカシュアイアクリスエローで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1253年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll(은)는 대략 1220년 영국 랭카실르 클리더에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1253년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll nasceu aproximadamente 1220, em Clitheroe, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu após 1253.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll родился около 1220 г. в местности клитиро, ланкашир, Англия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер после 1253 г.

es.lifeSummary: Richard de Knoll nació aproximadamente 1220, en Clitheroe, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió después de 1253.
